title: Review of Neon Genesis Evangelion The Unofficial Guide
display_title: Review of Neon Genesis Evangelion The Unofficial Guide
wikitext:
[]: Review of Neon Genesis Evangelion: The Unofficial Guide             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 20, 2004 7:12 pm Being the very first item I got supplied for review by a company, I was quite excited and eager about the opportunity to review and promote this book. I say "was" because I am no longer eager to sing any praise for the authors of this book. I thank publications director Clive France greatly for his time and assistance in reviewing this book. But unfortunately, I simply cannot say a single good thing about this book. A month or so back I recieved proofs of the book. I was instantly aware of numerous errors. Many of them were simple publishing errors... grammar, spelling, etc. However there were other errors so incredibly ludicrous and agregious that I questioned the competancy of the writers. The faults and failures of this book can probably be summed up by the following three reasons. 1. The writing of this book was most likely rushed. 2. The writers aren't that exceptionally skilled at writing. 3. The writers don't know a flip about Eva, and are simply writing because they are paid to do so. I'll give you a small taste of a few of the errors that irritated me. And I use the term "irritated" here liberally. Actually... I think I'll put it into context for you. I was so "irritated" with this book while reading it initially that I simply couldn't go any further. I was too thouroghly agravated and frustrated that I tossed it in a corner for a week and refused to read it. Ok, so here are some errors within the book. The authors suggest that LCL can melt objects. They list Kensuke as the Fourth Child and pilot of Eva 03. They refer to the AT Field at times as an "AC Field". They at serveral points confuse Unit numbers. Ramiel is refered to as an octagon, rather than an Octahedron. The Authors confuse Central and Terminal Dogma at several points. The authors refer to the First Impact as the extinction of the dinosaurs. Etc, etc, etc. And these are just a few of the errors from 30 or so pages of a 180 page book. And those are only the obvious ones, there are numerous errors related to understanding and comprehension. And then there are the errors of just plain bad judgement, such as stating that the key reason for Evangelion's success was the female characters appealing to "stay at home otaku". In fact, the psychological concepts, religious influence, references to older anime, and inner demons of director Anno were completely ignored. Speaking of ignored... the authors obviously never read Anno's confession letter which spelled out Shinji's character. In fact, their analysis of Shinji runs completely counter to Anno's (and other production members') explanation of Shinji's character. I simply cannot endorse this book. It simply serves to purport bogus and debunked theories. If you're a newer fan, you'll simply become more confused with the Eva series. If you're an older fan, you'll simply be iritated. In either case, your money would probably be better spent on something else. Take it from me. I could barely get through this book because of the sheer pain it inspired. Do not buy this book. You have been warned.

[]: Re: Review of Neon Genesis Evangelion: The Unofficial Guide             Quote Post by Quiddity » Tue Jul 20, 2004 8:07 pm As I've said before, our discussions at boards like this are far more valuable discussions and much more informed than any book like this ever could be. Your review confirms what I expected, I hadn't planned on buying it before and I will stay with that assessment now. How unfortunate that the so called 'experts', people 'in the industry' like Amanda Winn, writers for mags like Animerica, and the writers of this book are completely ignorant about far too much of Evangelion, too often spouting off their WRONG opinions and poorly thought up rumors(First Impact=Dinosaurs e.g.) as facts. And then there are the errors of just plain bad judgement, such as stating that the key reason for Evangelion's success was the female characters appealing to "stay at home otaku". In fact, the psychological concepts, religious influence, references to older anime, and inner demons of director Anno were completely ignored.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Tue Jul 20, 2004 8:20 pm Aside from Quiddity's line about window dressing religious overtones (which we are discussing in another topic), I agree with him. Your fanaticism is showing Mr. Monkey. Of course it's popular because it has hot babes. Luckily that ain't the only reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Tue Jul 20, 2004 9:20 pm Now I can't shake off the idea that some poor idiot is going to read this book and actually take it seriously. :(

[]: Re: Review of Neon Genesis Evangelion: The Unofficial Guide             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Tue Jul 20, 2004 9:21 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I simply cannot say a single good thing about this book.

[]:             Quote Post by Quiddity » Tue Jul 20, 2004 10:30 pm Phaze wrote: Now I can't shake off the idea that some poor idiot is going to read this book and actually take it seriously. :(

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 20, 2004 10:46 pm Quiddity wrote: Meaning many more will be reading crap like that than reading messages on a place like this.

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Tue Jul 20, 2004 10:57 pm this stuff is cheap and worthless to me i want to learn about evangelion through watching it and discussing it with good people. not by reading some bullshit book

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 20, 2004 11:17 pm ShoKenju wrote: i want to learn about evangelion through watching it and discussing it with good people. not by reading some bullshit book

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Tue Jul 20, 2004 11:20 pm Why don't you make your own Evangelion book Mr. Monkey? Or are you alreadying doing that...?

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Tue Jul 20, 2004 11:29 pm I would buy it, thats for sure. I wonder though... would he ever quote us? His loyal Underlings?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 20, 2004 11:42 pm I actually am in pre-planning right now. I figure, if this book, as bad it is, can be published, anything can... even something written by me. I plan to apply for an undergraduate research award in the winter. They quite often GIVE these things away at my school. Hell, one girl got a $1500 award to research the importance of female detectives in contemporary british literature. Needless to say, prospects look good.

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Wed Jul 21, 2004 11:18 am I would buy it! You'd just have to be very careful not to find the irony and get reviews like the shitty one you just read. But would you use a lot of your essays and stuff from these forums in it possibly?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 21, 2004 4:14 pm Content from the site may not necessarily be reprinted within, but the concepts and ideas would be incorporated into my overall understanding, and the writing would reflect that. Even if I don't agree, I intend to offer theories, and reasons for and against things in an impartial and well reasoned manner.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Wed Jul 21, 2004 5:50 pm The cover "art" told me right away that it wouldn't be worth shit. If they can't spend enough time/money to make a decent looking cover, I won't bother reading.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Thu Jul 22, 2004 4:47 pm I just want to say thanks for the heads up, this way I can save some money to buy other eva stuff that is actually worth it.

[]:             Quote Post by Crazyman42 » Thu Jul 22, 2004 7:26 pm Even though I never buy Unofficial guides, thanks for the heads up. Though LCL milting classic objects! That's a classic.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Jul 23, 2004 6:45 pm Actually, I think this is one of the best things that could happen. Monkey's book could be even further propelled by the sheer half-assery of this book in the way that it now has the ability to call itself a "true" guide. Sure, some sap will read this piece of shit and believe most of what it says (he certainly can't believe everything in the book because plenty is disproven simply by watching the anime or reading the manga), but if he's going to buy a guide before he sees the real thing, I don't think there's any big loss that can't be redeemed by the advent of a well-written guide. If this had been an almost-flawless book, then those flaws would have had even more staying power. Not to mention that if Aaron gets cracking (or ADV, whichever takes longer), the guide could coincide with the release of the film, which most certainly couldn't do anything bad for sales. Aaron, are you planning an encyclopedia-style guide, or do you think you'll section it off by element or continuity? --- EDIT You know, something seems fishy about all of this. How can a book be that innacurate about anything and still get published? Are you sure they didn't just send you a revised crap version to annoy you? Even the cover looks more sarcastic than sad.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 23, 2004 9:33 pm The pre-publish proofs I was sent are identical to the published version I bought off of Amazon. It really is that bad. I had to buy it off of Amazon because otherwise I would have had to wait a month to recieve a copy via the company's distributor. I couldn't stand by and wait while innocent Eva fans purchased an enema in paperback form. Define "encyclopedic" Hexon.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Jul 24, 2004 1:56 am A book which contains a lot of terms in alphabetical order, with no consideration given to relevance, continuity, etc.. I think the word I was looking for was a dictionary format; just text, text, and more text. Those are boring.
